plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-615 RP20.P02.M04.S19-RP20.P02.M06.S14 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-615 covers the RP20 Data Room And VDR P02 secondary workflow test tail, full permission/audit binding descriptor, and synthetic fixture head over 40 planned units, preserving no-write, no-real-data, permission/audit, fixture head, test, tenant/matter, and handoff boundaries without opening Data Room or VDR runtime behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-615 (RP20 Data Room And VDR P02 permission/audit binding + synthetic fixture head, Risk B, 40 units, range RP20.P02.M04.S19-RP20.P02.M06.S14) passes independent read-only review with zero findings. Verified through Read/Grep/Glob only (no Bash, no edits, no writes, no connectors). (1) Coverage: registry decomposes the pack as M04=4 (P02_SERVICE_TEST_TAIL_ROWS), M05=22 (P02_SERVICE_REVIEW_ROWS = 20 service rows + review/integration tail), M06=14 (P02_SERVICE_ROWS.slice(0,14)) = 40 units / 3 sections; deliverable distribution (implementation 17, contract 2, security_audit 4, ui 5, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 2, test 8) is identical across the registry constant, requirements expected_deliverable_counts, manifest delivery_counts, the hand-mapped manifest included_units, and the prompt baseline. (2) No-write/no-real-data boundaries: freezeCp615Result freezes a descriptor with ~40 runtime/dispatch/write/permission/audit/storage and no-leak flags all false (including the CP615-specific executes_permission_audit_binding_runtime and executes_fixture_head_runtime), synthetic_only/no_real_data true, customer_safe_errors_only true; validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp615... asserts every one of those flags false plus the authority boundary; no filesystem, network, DB, or process I/O exists in any source/test/validator — they are pure frozen-object builders. (3) Tests assert covered_units 40, section_count 3, deliverable_counts deepEqual, production_ready_candidate true, all execute_* false, no_write_attestation reference-equality, hermes emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false, claude_is_final_approval false, handoff to CP00-616/RP20.P02.M06.S15, and deepEqual of contract.projections.cp615_{pack_binding,requirements,no_write_attestation} and contract.packs.cp615_p02_permission_audit_fixture_head_descriptor against generated code. (4) Contract and live plan are consistent: program block current_pack_id CP00-615 / next CP00-616 / current_range matches; manifest status in_progress, production_ready false, runtime_ready false, descriptor_only true, deviation_from_plan false. (5) Authority boundary holds: Claude is read_only_independent_reviewer and never final approver, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust false, allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence wired into the claude packet. (6) Hermes appears only as descriptor evidence-packet/receipt-shape metadata with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false (no product runtime). CP178+ hardening evidence is sound: auth preflight passed with non-zero valid-JSON stdout; the pending_review states in claude-review-result.json, construction-inspection.json, adjudication.md, and the empty command-evidence command matrix are by-design placeholders for this read_only_claude_run_raw_capture step (downstream normalize/receipt_validate/adjudication/inspection/final_closeout_validation populate them), not defects; unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly excluded from pack scope. The pack correctly holds production_ready/runtime_ready false at the descriptor layer and weakens no production_ready gate.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-615 data room vdr core p02 permission audit fixture head descriptor pack
